title: bromus commutatus subsp. neglectus
intro: Bromus commutatus subsp. neglectus, commonly known as rough meadow brome, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with its roots and leaves containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The rough meadow brome has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. It is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, and can also be found in some dietary supplements, particularly those aimed at promoting digestive health and reducing inflammation.
